channel.title: Salads and Dressings on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Salads and Dressings on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Betty’s Caesar Salad Dressing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¾ cup salad oil
  • ¼ cup lemon juice
  • 1 egg
  • 2 garlic cloves
  • Salt and pepper

Instructions

Place these ingredients in a blender and blend thoroughly. Chill.

Iceberg lettuce or Romaine lettuce Croutons Bacon, cooked crispy and crumbled

Tear lettuce in bite-sized pieces into salad bowl. Add dressing, bacon bits and Caesar salad mix to taste. Top with croutons.

item.title: Broccoli Salad – (bill & Heather’s Cookbook)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup raisins
  • ½ cup shelled sunflower seeds
  • 1 medium red onion, chopped
  • ½ lb crisp, crumbled bacon
  • 3 heads broccoli florets

Instructions

Combine the above ingredients in a large bowl.

Dressing: ½ cup Miracle Whip 2 tsp rice vinegar 1/3 cup white sugar (or Splenda)

Combine and add to broccoli mixture. Stir well and chill.

item.title: Caesar Salad Dressing – No. 2
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 egg yolks
  • ½ clove garlic
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ¼ cup grated Parmesan cheese
  • 2 tsp Worcestershire sauce
  • 2/3 cup olive oil

Instructions

Combine all ingredients in a blender. Mix well. Serve with romaine lettuce, croutons and bacon. Makes 1-1/2 cup dressing.

item.title: Caesar Salad Seasoning Mix
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ tsp salt
  • ½ tsp ground pepper
  • ½ tsp dry mustard
  • 1 cup (1/4 lb) grated Romano
  • ½ tsp crushed oregano
  • or Parmesan cheese or a blend
  • ½ tsp M.S.G. (optional)
  • or Parmesan and Romano

Instructions

Mix all ingredients thoroughly. Store in a covered jar at room temperature. Use as the seasoning in Caesar Salad. It may also be sprinkled over other tossed salads and used as a topping for casseroles and creamed soups.

item.title: Carrot & Raisin Salad
item.description:
Instructions

Combine in a mixing bowl 2 cups shredded carrots ½ cup diced celery ½ cup raisins

Mix together 1/3 cup mayonnaise or salad dressing 1 Tbps vinegar

Toss lightly with two forks and turn into lettuce-lined serving bowl.

Yield: 4 to 6 servings.

item.title: Cole Slaw Dressing
item.description:
Instructions

If you have a microwave and need a nice potato salad or cole slaw dressing in a hurry, here is one. In a medium glass bowl, with wire whip or rotary beater, thoroughly combine

½ cup water 1/3 cup vinegar ¼ to 1/3 cup sugar 1 egg 1 tbsp flour ½ tsp salt ½ tsp dry mustard

Heat on high 4 – 5 minutes until mixture is thickened, stirring twice; stir in

item.title: Coleslaw
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 qts finely shredded cabbage
  • 1 tbsp coarse salt (salad salt)
  • 1 cup peeled and shredded carrot
  • ½ cup mayonnaise
  • ½ cup commercial sour cream
  • 1 tsp celery salt
  • 1 tbsp vinegar
  • Salt and pepper
  • 3 tsp sugar

Instructions

In a large colander over a large bowl, layer the shredded cabbage and coarse salt. Place a plate over the cabbage and weight it down with a heavy object. Allow cabbage to stand at room temperature for at least 2 hours. Press out as much moisture as possible and place cabbage and the shredded carrot in a large bowl. In a small bowl blend the mayonnaise, sour cream, celery salt, sugar and vinegar. Add the mayonnaise mixture to the cabbage and stir until well combined. Chill for several hours or overnight. Taste the coleslaw after it has been chilled and adjust the seasoning if necessary.

item.title: Cooked Salad Dressing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 heaping tsp cornstarch
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 1 cup vinegar and 4 tsp water
  • 1 level tsp dry mustard
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 4 tbsp canned milk

Instructions

Combine above in double boiler and cook. After it starts to thicken, boil for 5 minutes. Chill before using.

item.title: Emergency Salad Dressing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • (Tastes lie Kraft Catalina)
  • 1 tsp salt
  • l tsp paprika
  • l/3 cup sugar
  • 2 tbsp lemon juice
  • l/4 cup vinegar
  • 2/3 cup salad oil
  • l/3 cup catsup (ketchup)

Instructions

Measure ingredients into bottle or jar. Cover tightly and shake until well mixed. Makes l-l/4 cups.

NOTE: Catsup is easily measured if you measure the oil then add the catsup to make l cup. When measured this way, the catsup doesn’t stick to the measuring cup.

item.title: Greek Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ head lettuce, torn bite size
  • 1 Romaine lettuce
  • 2 tomatoes, cut bite size
  • 1 cucumber, cut bite size
  • ¼ cup sliced green onions
  • ¼ cup sliced ripe olives
  • ¾ cup Feta cheese, cubed
  • ½ cup salad oil
  • ¼ cup red wine vinegar
  • ¼ cup chopped parsley
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1/8 tsp pepper
  • 1/8 tsp oregano
  • 1/8 tsp garlic powder

Instructions

Layer lettuce in large salad bowl or on a large platter. Spread with tomatoes, cucumber, onions, olives and cheese. Beat next seven ingredients together well. Shake before serving, Serve with salad.

item.title: Liz’s Cabbage Slaw
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 cups shredded cabbage
  • 1 cup shredded carrot
  • ¼ cup chopped green pepper
  • 1 cup mayonnaise
  • 2 Tbsp sugar
  • 2 Tbsp vinegar
  • 1 tsp prepared mustard
  • 1 tsp celery seed
  • ½ tsp salt

Instructions

Combine veggies and chill. Mix with slaw mix and toss well.

Serves 12.

item.title: Melody Dressing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp paprika
  • 1/3 cup sugar
  • 2 Tbsp lemon juice
  • ¼ cup vinegar
  • 1/3 cup ketchup
  • ½ cup Canola oil

Instructions

Measure all ingredients into a bottle or jar.

Cover tightly and shake well, chill thoroughly.

Shake well before serving.

Yield: 1-1/4 cups.

item.title: Patty Bartlett’s Noodle Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 85-gr pkg Ramen noodles
  • 3 Tbsp vinegar
  • (the ones w/the flavour packets)
  • 1 Tbsp sugar
  • 1 cup almonds
  • 2 cups cooked chicken
  • 2 Tbsp butter
  • 2 green onions, chopped
  • ½ cup oil
  • 1 small cabbage, shredded

Instructions

Drop noodles into boiling water for 1 to 1-1/2 minutes. Drain and cool. Saute almonds in butter until toasted. Toss all ingredients together.

Sounds weird; tastes good!

item.title: Raspberry Vinaigrette (community Voices)
item.description:
Instructions

INGREDIENTS 1 cup virgin olive oil ½ cup raspberry vinegar 2 tsp dry mustard 3 Tbsp dried , finely crushed onion flakes 4 Tbsp liquid honey (more or less to suit taste) Fresh herbs to taste (basil, dill, mint or your choice) Salt & pepper to taste

DIRECTIONS Put ingredients in a bottle and shake well before using. Store dressing in fridge.

This vinaigrette is wonderful sprinkled on green salads or dribbled over steamed vegetables.

item.title: Raspberry Vinegar #2 (community Voices)
item.description:
Instructions

If you like your summer salads light and refreshing, all you need to do s splash on a dash of raspberry vinegar. Sprinkle it straight from the bottle or use it as a base for Raspberry Vinaigrette.

INGREDIENTS: 5 cups apple cider vinegar (or red wine vinegar) 5 handfuls of raspberries, washed

DIRECTIONS: Put berries into bottle or jar. Heat vinegar until boiling point. Pour over raspberries, leaving about one inch head space. Cork or cap and set in a cool dark place for several days before using.

item.title: Salad Dressing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 can Eagle Brand milk
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 can vinegar
  • salt and pepper
  • 1 tsp dry mustard

Instructions

Mix 2 eggs, add Eagle Brand milk and dry mustard; mix thoroughly. Then add salt and pepper to taste. Mix thoroughly and add 1 can vinegar. This is an excellent dressing for potato salad and will keep in the fridge for quite a while.

item.title: Seven-up Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 can 7-Up
  • ½ bag marshmallows
  • 1 large pkg lime Jello
  • 1 19-oz can crushed pineapple
  • 8 oz pkg cream cheese
  • 1 pkg Dream Whip

Instructions

Dissolve over low heat marshmallows in one can of 7-Up. Add lime Jello and stir until dissolved. Add cream cheese and beat until smooth. Add pineapple. When partially set, beat Cream Whip and fold in. Mold and chill.

item.title: Speedy Bean Salad - Cw
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¾ cup sugar
  • 2/3 cup vinegar
  • 1/3 cup vegetable oil
  • 1 tsp salt
  • Dash pepper
  • 1 15-1/2 oz kidney beans, rinsed &
  • 1 15-oz garbanzo beans, rinsed &
  • drained
  • drained
  • ½ medium onion, chopped
  • 1 15-oz great northern beans, rinsed
  • ½ cup chopped green pepper
  • & drained

Instructions

In a bowl, combine sugar, vinegar, oil, salt and pepper. Stir in all remaining ingredients. Let marinate until serving time.

Yield: 6 servings

item.title: Spinach Salad W/strawberry & Poppy Seed Dressing
item.description:
Instructions

To prepare dressing, whisk together 1/3 cup mayonnaise 2 Tbsp milk 2 Tbsp sugar 1 Tbsp white wine vinegar 1 Tbsp poppy seed 1/8 tsp salt

Combine in a bowl 8 cups baby spinach or torn romaine lettuce 2 cups sliced strawberries ¼ cup slivered almonds, toasted

Toss with dressing to coat. Serve immediately. Serves 8.

item.title: Wendy Coleslaw Dressing
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 cups finely shredded cabbage
  • 1 cup finely shredded carrot
  • 1 cup mayonnaise
  • ½ cup milk
  • ½ cup minced onion
  • ¼ cup sugar
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 2 drops hot pepper sauce (optional)
  • 1/8 tsp pepper
  • ½ tsp celery salt

Instructions

Toss cabbage and carrot. Mix all other ingredients in a separate bowl. Toss with large fork until well coated. Refrigerate for at least 1-1/2 hr. before serving.

Makes one quart of coleslaw.
